title: The effect of speech masking on the human subcortical response to continuous speech
description: This dataset comprises auditory brainstem response (ABR) recordings from 25 normal-hearing participants exposed to continuous speech under varying masking conditions. Participants listened to peaky speech from one to five simultaneous talkers at different signal-to-noise ratios (clean, 0 dB, -3 dB, -6 dB SNR), as well as click trains, while EEG was recorded. The study investigates how speech masking affects subcortical neural encoding of naturally produced speech in human listeners.
methods_description: EEG recordings were collected from 25 participants (ages 19-37 years, mean ± SD: 23.4 ± 5.5) with normal hearing (audiometric thresholds ≤20 dB HL from 500-8000 Hz). Stimuli included randomized click trains at 40 Hz (60 × 10 s trials) and peaky speech from male narrators at four SNR conditions (clean, 0 dB, -3 dB, -6 dB), each presented for 30 minutes at 65 dB SPL through ER-2 insert earphones at 48 kHz sampling rate. Participants sat in a sound-isolating booth while resting or watching silent captioned videos. EEG was recorded in Brain Products format and preprocessed using custom Python scripts.
